free_text: Measurement of double differential neutron emission
cross sections at 14.1 MeV for Ti, Mo, Sn and Sb
Preliminary data for ADX and EDX given for Ti, Mo, Sn
(Elastic ADX is probably misprinted for Ti.)
2 cm diam tritiated titanium target, pulsed
beam with 1.8 nsec pulse width and 1 MHz repetition
.Cylindrical natural metal rod 3.0 cm in diam and
.Monitor sample was polyethylene cylinder, 1.5 cm in
Neutron flight path length of about 8.3 m
fixed at 85 deg to the deuteron beam line.
NE-213 liquid scintillator 25.4 cm in diameter
and 10.0 cm in length. The detector was equipped with
pulse shape discrimination for gamma-ray rejection.
Source neutron flux was monitored by another NE-213
scintillator with 5.08 cm diam 5.08 cm length set at
0 deg to the deuteron beam line.
!  with the help of MUSCC3 code, see REL-REF,
based on JENDL-3. In case that JENDL-3 would produce
double differential neutron emission spectrum in
drastic disagreement with the measured one, the
correction can be skipped and the adoption of raw,
i.e. experimental, data is recommended by authors.
.This correction amounted to about 10%, except for
backward elastic scattering where it could be
.Correction factor are calculated using evaluated data,
however, they are still primitive. Calculation of cor-
rection factors should be carried out again after new
version of evaluation would be published.*By authors*
Errors do not contain detector efficiency
